name: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
description:
Given an array of integers arr and an integer k, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.

A sliding window is a conceptual window that moves across the array. For this problem, the window size is fixed at k. We calculate the sum of elements within the current window and then slide the window one position to the right, updating the sum efficiently.

Example:

arr = [2, 1, 5, 1, 3, 2]
k = 3

Steps:

  • Initialize a window of size k (first 3 elements): [2, 1, 5]. Sum = 8.
  • Slide the window one position to the right: [1, 5, 1]. To get the new sum, subtract the element leaving the window (2) and add the new element entering the window (1). New sum = 8 - 2 + 1 = 7.
  • Slide again: [5, 1, 3]. Sum = 7 - 1 + 3 = 9.
  • Slide again: [1, 3, 2]. Sum = 9 - 5 + 3 = 7.

The maximum sum found is 9.
